name: Home Style Chicken
recipeIngredient: 2 chicken legs with thighs (1 to 1.25 pounds each)
Wine, Chinese rice (1 tablespoon) or Sherry
Spinach leaves and stems removed (3 cups)
recipeInstructions:
1. Hold each chicken portion by the end of a leg with a kitchen towel. Pull off the skin at the thigh end and discard it.
2. Use a sharp boning or paring knife to slit the meat on the legs and thighs parallel to the bone, scraping the meat away from the bone. Cut through the joint between the leg and thigh. Remove all remaining meat and bones; cut or pull out the bones.
3. Place each boned piece of chicken, skin side down, on a work surface. Break up tendons by scoring the meat with a cleaver.
4. Cut the meat into 1½-inch pieces and transfer to a small bowl. Sprinkle with salt, pepper, and wine or sherry. Stir in beaten egg, cornstarch, and toss to combine. Drizzle with oil, toss, cover, and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
5. For Sauce: In a small bowl, stir together a little stock and cornstarch. Add remaining sauce ingredients and set aside.
6. For Finishing Sauce: Blend all ingredients in a small bowl and set aside.
7. Heat oil until nearly smoking in a wok or large skillet. Add spinach, chicken stock, garlic, and salt; stir-fry until just wilted (1 to 1½ minutes). Arrange on a warm serving plate.
8. Heat oil to 360°F (garlic sizzles steadily when placed in the oil). Add chicken pieces, separating them with a skimmer; fry turning once or twice until lightly golden, about 2–3 minutes. Remove and drain on paper towels.
9. Raise heat of oil to 400°F and return chicken to the wok. Fry until golden brown, about 1 minute, then remove and drain.
10. Pour off all but 1 tablespoon of oil. Add garlic, scallions, and ginger; cook for about 30 seconds.
11. Add sauce, then chicken; toss to coat. Add finishing mixture; tossing to combine.
12. Arrange chicken on spinach and serve immediately.
